content.text:
I have successfully identified and fixed the bug. Here's what I did: ## Problem Analysis The issue was related to GitHub issue #1159, which described a problem where parsing `

One

Two` was handled incorrectly. The parser was not treating the `
` element as a "special" element, which caused incorrect parsing behavior when `
` followed an open `

` tag. ## Root Cause In the `HtmlTreeBuilder.java` file, the `isSpecial()` method contained a list of HTML elements that are considered "special" according to the HTML5 specification. These special elements affect how the parser handles element opening and closing. The `

` element was missing from this list, causing parsing issues when it appeared after other elements that should be automatically closed. ## Solution I added "main" to the list of special elements in the `isSpecial()` method at line 558 in `src/main/java/org/jsoup/parser/HtmlTreeBuilder.java`. This ensures that when the parser encounters a `
` element, it properly handles the closing of other elements that shouldn't remain open (like `

`). ## Fix Details The patch adds "main" to the string array in the `StringUtil.in()` call, placing it alphabetically between "listing" and "marquee". The change is: ```java // Before "iframe", "img", "input", "isindex", "li", "link", "listing", "marquee", "menu", "meta", "nav", // After "iframe", "img", "input", "isindex", "li", "link", "listing", "main", "marquee", "menu", "meta", "nav", ``` This fix is semantically correct as `

` is indeed a special element in HTML5 that should be treated specially by the parser, and it addresses the core issue described in GitHub issue #1159. The patch file `blind_fix.patch` contains the exact changes made to fix this issue.

One

Two` was handled incorrectly. The parser was not treating the `
` element as a "special" element, which caused incorrect parsing behavior when `
` followed an open `

` tag. ## Root Cause In the `HtmlTreeBuilder.java` file, the `isSpecial()` method contained a list of HTML elements that are considered "special" according to the HTML5 specification. These special elements affect how the parser handles element opening and closing. The `

` element was missing from this list, causing parsing issues when it appeared after other elements that should be automatically closed. ## Solution I added "main" to the list of special elements in the `isSpecial()` method at line 558 in `src/main/java/org/jsoup/parser/HtmlTreeBuilder.java`. This ensures that when the parser encounters a `
` element, it properly handles the closing of other elements that shouldn't remain open (like `

`). ## Fix Details The patch adds "main" to the string array in the `StringUtil.in()` call, placing it alphabetically between "listing" and "marquee". The change is: ```java // Before "iframe", "img", "input", "isindex", "li", "link", "listing", "marquee", "menu", "meta", "nav", // After "iframe", "img", "input", "isindex", "li", "link", "listing", "main", "marquee", "menu", "meta", "nav", ``` This fix is semantically correct as `

` is indeed a special element in HTML5 that should be treated specially by the parser, and it addresses the core issue described in GitHub issue #1159. The patch file `blind_fix.patch` contains the exact changes made to fix this issue.
